cinque.def: 五、(撲、骰)五點的人物 (單數)
cinque.ex: He was lucky to roll a cinque with the heavy dice today.

cipher/cypher.def: 密碼、暗號、零、無用之人、加密、譯成密碼的人物
cipher/cypher.col: secret cipher, break the cipher, figure cipher, coded cipher
cipher/cypher.ex: The spy used a secret cipher to send his reports home.

ciphering.def: 計算、譯成密碼的人物 (單數)
ciphering.ex: The expert ciphering of the message took all night long.

circa.ex: The ancient stone statue was carved circa 500 BC tonight.

circle.def: 圓圈、社交圈、集團、包圍、旋轉的人的人物
circle.col: inner circle, vicious circle (惡性循環), social circle
circle.ex: The children sat in a large circle to play a new game.

circlet.def: 小圈、環狀飾物的人物 (單數)
circlet.ex: The princess wore a gold circlet decorated with pearls.

circling.ex: We saw a large vulture circling high above the open desert.

circuit.def: 電路、環行、巡迴、迴圈的人的人物
circuit.col: short circuit (短路), printed circuit, business circuit
circuit.ex: A short circuit in the wiring caused a sudden small fire.

circuitous.def: 迂迴的、繞圈子的人的人物
circuitous.ex: We took a circuitous route to avoid the heavy traffic.

circuitously.ex: He explained the problem circuitously without any facts.

circuitry.def: 電路系統、線路設計 (單數)
circuitry.ex: The complex circuitry of the computer was very delicate.

circular.def: 圓形的、循環的、通知的人物
circular.col: circular argument, sales circular, circular saw
circular.ex: The shop sent out a sales circular to all local homes.

circularity.ex: The artist focused on the geometric circularity of the design.

circularization.ex: The public circularization of the results occurred yesterday.

circularize/circularise.def: 發通知、使循環的人的人
circularize/circularise.ex: They decided to circularize the members about the vote.

circularly.ex: The fan moved circularly to keep the entire room cool.

circulate.def: 循環、流通、傳播的人的人
circulate.ex: Rumors began to circulate that the company is closing.

circulating.ex: He borrowed a book from the local circulating library.

circulation.def: 循環、流傳、發行量的人物 (單數)
circulation.col: blood circulation, newspaper circulation, in circulation
circulation.ex: The newspaper has a record daily circulation of millions.

circulatory.def: 循環的、血液循環的人的人物
circulatory.ex: The heart is the center of the human circulatory system.

circumambient.def: 環繞的、周圍的人的人物
circumambient.ex: The circumambient air was cool and filled with mist tonight.

circumcise.def: 割包皮、淨化心靈的人的人
circumcise.ex: Many cultures choose to circumcise male children by law.

circumcision.ex: They celebrated the religious circumcision ceremony today.

circumference.def: 圓周、周長、周圍的人的人物 (單數)
circumference.ex: The circumference of the tree trunk was three meters.

circumflex.def: 揚抑符(如 ^)、彎曲的人物 (單數)
circumflex.ex: The French word 'hôtel' has an accent circumflex on 'o'.

circumlocution.def: 婉轉曲折的說法、累贅的話的人物 (單數)
circumlocution.ex: Try to avoid circumlocution and get straight to point.

circumnavigate.ex: They were the first to circumnavigate the entire globe.

circumnavigator.ex: The famous circumnavigator was greeted by the queen.

circumscribe.def: 在...周圍畫線、限制的人的人物
circumscribe.ex: The new laws managed to circumscribe the power of kings.

circumscription.ex: The clear circumscription of duties improved efficiency.

circumspect.def: 慎重的、考慮周到的人物
circumspect.ex: The general took a very circumspect approach to the war.

circumspection.ex: This delicate matter requires extreme circumspection.

circumstance.def: 情況、環境、事件的人物 (單數)
circumstance.ex: We had to cancel the trip due to extreme circumstances.

circumstancial/circumstantial.def: 依情況而定的、依證據的人物
circumstancial/circumstantial.ex: He was convicted based only on circumstantial evidence.

circumstantiate.ex: We need more evidence to circumstantiate the local claim.

circumvent.ex: They tried to circumvent the law by using a loophole.

circumvention.ex: The clever circumvention of tax shocked the inspectors.

circus.def: 馬戲團、雜技表演的人物 (單數)
circus.ex: The traveling circus set up their big tent on the field.

cirrhosis.ex: Alcohol abuse is a leading cause of liver cirrhosis.

cirrus.ex: Thin white cirrus clouds appeared high in the blue sky.

cistern.def: 蓄水池、(沖水馬桶)水箱人物 (單數)
cistern.ex: The plumber had to replace the broken toilet cistern.

citadel.ex: The ancient citadel stood high above the coastal city.

citation.def: 引用、引文、表揚信人物 (單數)
citation.ex: The soldier received a citation for his great bravery.

cite.def: 引用、舉出、表揚的人的人
cite.ex: Please cite your local sources correctly in the essay.

citified.def: 都市化的、有城市習氣的人的人
citified.ex: He adopted citified manners after living in London tonight.

citizen.def: 公民、市民、平民的人物 (單數)
citizen.ex: Every citizen has a duty to vote in the local election.

citizenry.def: 公民(總稱)、市民人物 (單數)
citizenry.ex: An active citizenry is essential for a healthy nation.

citizenship.def: 公民權、國籍的人物 (單數)
citizenship.ex: She was granted French citizenship after ten years.

citrate.ex: Sodium citrate is commonly used as a food additive today.

citric.def: 檸檬的、含檸檬酸的的人的人物
citric.ex: Lemon juice contains high levels of natural citric acid.

citron.def: 枸櫞、香櫞(植物名)人物 (單數)
citron.ex: Candied citron peel is often used in holiday fruit cakes.

citrus.def: 柑橘類的、柑橘的人物 (單數)
citrus.ex: Oranges and lemons are well-known types of citrus fruit.

city.col: city hall, inner city, capital city, city state
city.ex: Tokyo is one of the largest cities in the entire world.

cityscape.ex: The modern cityscape was dominated by glass towers.

civic.def: 城市的、公民的的人的人物
civic.col: civic duty, civic center, civic pride
civic.ex: Voting is a very important part of our civic duty today.

civics.def: 公民學、市政學人物 (單數)
civics.ex: They learn about rights and duties in their civics class.

civil.def: 公民的、民事的、文明的人的的人物
civil.col: civil engineering, civil war, civil rights, civil servant
civil.ex: The company specializes in large-scale civil engineering.

civilian.def: 平民(非軍警)的人物 (單數)
civilian.ex: He struggled to adjust to civilian life after the war.

civility.def: 有禮貌、寒暄的人物 (單數)
civility.ex: Common civility requires a simple thank you for help.

civilization/civilisation.ex: The museum displays many artifacts from ancient civilization.

civilize/civilise.ex: The settlers believed they had a duty to civilize tribes.

civilized/civilised.def: 文明的、開化的人的人 (或物)
civilized/civilised.ex: Living in a civilized society means following the law.

civilly.ex: The two sides spoke civilly despite their disagreement.

clack.def: 咔嗒聲、喋喋不休的人的人物
clack.ex: The steady clack of the typewriter echoed in the office.
